creative.concept: Use-case tip: 'Your journal writes itself.' — 3-step how-it-works card for a personal journaling angle. Pivots the week away from developer-heavy content (commits, PRs, standups, stat cards) toward a broader lifestyle audience on Sunday. Red circle on step 1 is the only color pop; format last used 15 days ago (Jun 13).

cards.title: Use-case tip: Your journal writes itself
cards.use: Primary visual for LinkedIn and X. Export as 1080×1080 PNG. The headline is the hook; numbered steps make the workflow immediately legible to someone who has never heard of Jot.

storyboard.title: Morning journaling — spoken in 2 minutes
storyboard.use: Instagram Reel / X video / GIF — personal writing hook; pivots from developer clips toward a broader Sunday lifestyle audience; pairs naturally with today's journaling card

shots.visual: Apple Notes open, blank page, cursor blinking after a date stamp — the usual blank-stare moment
Hand presses ⌥ Space; Dynamic Island-style pill appears pulsing under Mac notch
Pill shows pulsing audio waveform; user speaks a morning reflection — thoughts, plans, gratitude; words fill Notes in real time
Full paragraph fills Notes; pill vanishes; jot-transcribe.com watermark lower-right

drafts.text:
Most people journal for 5 minutes then quit — because typing is slow and kills the momentum.

I press ⌥ Space and speak instead. My thoughts flow at conversation speed, land in Notes or Pages, and I'm done in 2 minutes.

Jot: free, on-device Mac dictation. Any app, any text field, nothing leaves your Mac.

What's one habit you'd do more of if the friction was gone?

#macOS #productivity #indiedev
drafts.note: Attach morning-journal-tip.svg (export as 1080×1080 PNG). Do NOT put the link in the post body — LinkedIn downranks posts with external links. Paste the utm_url as the FIRST COMMENT immediately after posting. Closing question is the engagement trigger — answer every reply. Note: Sunday is LinkedIn's weakest day; if impressions matter more than consistency, hold this for Monday 7am PT instead.

drafts.text: Typing kills journaling momentum. Jot: press ⌥ Space, speak your morning thoughts, done in 2 minutes. Free, on-device Mac dictation — nothing leaves your Mac.
drafts.note: Attach morning-journal-tip.svg (export as PNG). No link in post — post the utm_url as the first reply immediately after posting. ~159 characters, within the 200-char limit.

drafts.text:
I started speaking my journal instead of typing it — here's what changed after 30 days

I'd tried journaling dozens of times. The sticking point was always the same: typing out thoughts slowly felt like editing while thinking, and by sentence two I'd lose the thread.

So I started dictating instead with Jot — press a hotkey, speak, and the transcript pastes wherever your cursor is.

What actually changed:
- My entries are 3× longer. I write the way I talk — complete thoughts, not compressed bullets.
- The habit actually stuck. Friction was the real block, not motivation.
- I'm surprised by what comes out. When I type, I self-censor. When I speak, I say what I mean.

Jot pastes directly into any app — Notes, Obsidian, Pages, Day One, anywhere. Transcription runs entirely on the Apple Neural Engine — no cloud, no account, no audio leaves your Mac.

Apple Silicon, macOS 15+. Free, open source.

(I built this — disclosure)

Link in comments.
drafts.note: Post to r/MacOS (rotation: macapps Jun 23, MacOS Jun 24, productivity Jun 25, MacOS Jun 26, macapps Jun 27, MacOS Jun 28). First line is the post title. Replace 'Link in comments' with the actual utm_url as the first comment. recommended:false — last recommended:true reddit was June 27 (1 day ago); needs 4+ days — earliest is July 1. Answer every comment in the first hour.

opportunities.title: Best Dictation Apps for Mac and MacBook (2026) — UseVoicy
opportunities.why: New roundup not previously seen in search history. High-SEO page comparing dictation apps; Jot almost certainly absent as the only fully-free open-source on-device option. Check if comments are enabled — if so, a single disclosure comment would reach high-intent shoppers.
opportunities.suggested_reply: One worth adding to this list: Jot (jot-transcribe.com) — free, open-source, on-device on the Apple Neural Engine. Hotkey → speak → text at cursor in any Mac app. No subscription, no account, no audio leaves your Mac. 11 languages as of v1.16. I built it. (Disclosure: developer.)

opportunities.title: Best Wispr Flow Alternatives for 2026 — ToolFinder
opportunities.why: New alternatives page surfaced today not previously flagged. Jot almost certainly absent. ToolFinder has interactive listing pages — submitting Jot here would surface it to users actively switching away from Wispr Flow.
opportunities.suggested_reply: Don't overlook Jot (jot-transcribe.com) — free, open-source Mac dictation that never sends audio off your Mac. Hotkey → speak → text at cursor in any app. Apple Neural Engine, 11 languages, no subscription. I built it. (Disclosure: developer.)

opportunities.title: Apple Dictation Review 2026: Is the Free Built-In Enough? — Voibe
opportunities.why: Article specifically calls out Apple Dictation's 30-second timeout and vocabulary limits — the exact pain points Jot solves. Voibe's blog likely has comments; a single honest note reaches users already frustrated with Apple Dictation who assume they must pay to upgrade.
opportunities.suggested_reply: If the 30-second timeout and vocabulary gaps are the frustration, Jot is worth trying — free, open-source Mac dictation on the Neural Engine with no timeout and custom vocab boosting. Works in any app. I built it. (Disclosure: developer.) jot-transcribe.com

notes: Downloads reached 237 today (+6 from June 27's 231; avg7=7/day). The 250-download milestone is 13 downloads away — likely Tuesday or Wednesday at current pace. iTunes API returned an error for the 16th consecutive day; appstore.json date updated to 2026-06-28, all other values preserved (ratingCount:4, avgRating:5, installs:0). Outreach window remains closed (last outreach June 24; reopens July 1 — priority: machow2.com, speakmac.app, Resonant 'What Reddit Recommends' page, and a first comment on the Voibe Superwhisper alternatives page if not yet done). Three new roundup pages surfaced today where Jot is absent: UseVoicy, ToolFinder Wispr Flow alternatives, and Voibe's Apple Dictation Review. Resonant continues to appear as the de-facto free/local recommendation in all roundups; no third-party page mentions Jot. Today's journaling card is the first personal/lifestyle angle after a seven-day developer run — it tests breadth of audience. Suggestion: hold the LinkedIn post for Monday 7am PT for maximum reach; use Sunday for comment opportunities on the three new pages above instead.
